title: Today's AI Brief: YouTube's AI Feed and Google's AI Plans
summary: YouTube introduces custom AI video feeds, while Google announces new AI subscriptions and features.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for YouTube will let you ask AI to make a custom video feed
imageCaption: YouTube will let you ask AI to make a custom video feed (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. I've got some fresh updates from the world of artificial intelligence. YouTube is now letting users ask AI to create a custom video feed, simply by describing the type of content they want to see. Imagine telling a friend, 'Hey, show me some workout guides and hobby inspiration videos,' and getting a personalized feed. That's what's happening here. This feature uses AI to curate videos based on user prompts, which could change how we discover content on the platform.

Meanwhile, Google has announced new AI subscriptions and features, fresh from their I/O 2026 event. They've introduced a $100 AI Ultra plan, along with new features and benefits for AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra users. It's interesting to see how these platforms are evolving, especially when it comes to AI-driven content and services. The boundaries of data, safety, and trust are always top of mind in these developments.

Another aspect to consider is how these AI-driven services will shape our daily interactions. For instance, YouTube's custom feed could make it easier to find content that resonates with us, but it also raises questions about how algorithms influence our viewing habits. And with Google's expanded AI offerings, we might see new applications across various industries.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
